# Wedding Planner / Celebrant — Nigeria-First Research Brief

**Niche ID:** P3-wedding-planner-wedding-planner-site
**Vertical slug:** wedding-planner
**VN-ID:** VN-PRO-EVT-001
**Niche family:** NF-PRO-EVT anchor (events professional family)
**Family role:** anchor
**Research date:** 2026-04-26

## 1. Nigerian Business Reality
Nigerian weddings are elaborate, multi-day affairs: introduction (wine carrying), traditional wedding (aso-ebi, traditional attire, family rites), white/church wedding, and reception. Planning a full Nigerian wedding typically costs ₦2M–₦50M+. Wedding planners coordinate venues, caterers, photographers, MCs, live bands, asoebi fabric vendors, cake designers, decorators, and travel logistics. The market is particularly strong in Lagos, Abuja, Port Harcourt, and the Diaspora-to-Nigeria destination wedding segment. Instagrammable content is a primary marketing channel. The CPPE (Council of Professional Party and Event Practitioners) is the industry body.

## 2. Target Audience Profile
- **Adaeze Okoye, 28, Bride-to-be, Lagos** — Planning a 300-guest wedding in 8 months. Needs a planner who understands traditional Igbo customs, has vendor network, delivers stress-free experience.
- **Mrs Funmilayo Adeyemi, 55, Mother of the Groom, Abuja** — Wants a formal, elegant event. Needs timeline management, protocol adherence, government guest coordination.
- **Dayo & Tolu, 32/30, Diaspora couple, London/Lagos** — Planning destination wedding in Lagos. Need full remote coordination, hotel block booking, aso-ebi management.

## 3. Recommended Website Structure
- **Home:** Hero (stunning Nigerian wedding image), packages overview, "Start Planning" CTA, testimonials
- **Services:** Full Planning, Day-of Coordination, Traditional Wedding Only, Destination Weddings, Decor & Styling
- **Portfolio/Gallery:** Real weddings — traditional, church, reception photography
- **Packages & Pricing:** Three tiers (Essential/Classic/Luxury) — NGN price ranges
- **About:** Planner profile, years of experience, CPPE membership, featured media
- **Contact:** WhatsApp, phone, enquiry form, NDPR notice

## 4. Content Tone & Voice
Aspirational, warm, deeply Nigerian. "Your perfect Nigerian wedding starts here" register. Reference Yoruba/Igbo/Hausa wedding traditions by name. Primary CTA: "Start Your Journey on WhatsApp". Secondary CTA: "View Our Packages". Avoid westernised stock imagery — centre authentic Nigerian wedding aesthetics.

## 5. Trust Signal Inventory
- CPPE membership badge
- CAC business registration
- Years of experience + number of weddings planned
- Featured in media (Bella Naija Weddings, Munaluchi Bride)
- Real wedding portfolio (with couple consent)
- Vendor network strength (top Lagos caterers, photographers)
- Payment: Paystack, bank transfer; milestone-based payment schedule

## 6. Image Direction (5-7 Specific Concepts)
1. Traditional wedding — couple in matching Yoruba aso-oke, kolanut ceremony, family in background
2. White wedding aisle — Nigerian church setting, flower arch, guests in aso-ebi
3. Reception decoration — lavish balloon arch, LED backdrop, Lagos hall setting
4. Bride preparation — hair, makeup, gele tying — warm natural light, Lagos home
5. Outdoor traditional setting — Igbo wine-carrying ceremony under decorated canopy
6. Wedding cake — elaborate tiered cake with Nigerian gold/royal blue motif
7. Planner in action — clipboard, headset, coordinating at a venue

## 7. Regulatory Compliance Checklist
- CAC business registration
- CPPE membership certificate display
- LASG/state event permit compliance notation
- NDPR consent for couple data (PII: names, date of event, guest list)
- Vendor contractual terms reference (force majeure, COVID/ASUU disruption clauses)

## 8. Nigeria-First Compliance Checklist
- [x] WhatsApp CTA on home page and contact page
- [x] All prices in NGN (₦) — package ranges
- [x] Nigerian cities and wedding contexts (Lagos, Abuja, Igbo/Yoruba/Hausa traditions)
- [x] Nigerian names (Adaeze, Funmilayo, Dayo) in testimonials
- [x] CPPE trust signal displayed
- [x] Payment: Paystack, bank transfer, milestone schedule
- [x] Mobile-first 375px layout
- [x] esc() on all user-supplied strings
- [x] NDPR notice in enquiry forms
